lines: Okay so tonight it's Paw Patrol, the one where the pups save the day, you ready?
Yeah! Chase and Marshall!
Right, so Ryder gets the call on his big watch thing, beep beep beep.
And he says no job is too big, no pup is too small.
Then what happens?
So all the puppies run up the slide into the big tower, the lookout.
Chase is the police one, he goes ready for action Ryder sir.
And Marshall is the firetruck one and he trips on the stairs like always.
Why does he always trip?
Because he's a little clumsy, that's why he's funny.
And Skye is the pink one, she flies in her helicopter, whoosh.
Rubble! He digs!
Yes, Rubble has the bulldozer, he likes to dig holes.
So a kitty got stuck up a tree in Adventure Bay.
And Mayor Goodway is all worried about her chicken Chickaletta.
So Chase and Skye go up and get the kitty down safe.
And everybody barks yay, mission complete.
The end, lie back down now.
Can we do another one?
No, one pup story, it's bedtime.
stories.tell_note: Franchise named aloud line 1 plus Ryder, Chase, Marshall, Skye, Rubble, Adventure Bay all spelled right; unmistakable.

lines: Lie down, I'll tell you the Paw Patrol one with the boat.
Zuma! Zuma has the boat!
Yes Zuma the chocolate lab, he says let's dive in.
So there's a big storm in Adventure Bay and Captain Turbot's boat gets stuck on the rocks.
Uh oh.
And he calls Ryder on the pup pad, help help.
So Ryder presses the badges and says Paw Patrol to the lookout.
Chase! And Marshall!
Well this time it's a water rescue so Zuma drives the hovercraft.
And Rocky comes too, he's the green one who fixes everything, reuse reduce recycle.
Then what about Skye?
Skye flies over in her helicopter to look for the boat from up high.
And she sees it, Captain Turbot waving his arms.
So Zuma drives out on the waves and throws him the buoy.
And Rocky fixes the hole in the boat with his tools.
Yay they did it!
Ryder says good job pups, and they all get a treat.
Okay close your eyes, mission complete.
stories.tell_note: Franchise named line 1; Zuma, Rocky, Chase, Marshall, Skye, Ryder, Captain Turbot, Adventure Bay all correct; obvious; differentiated from story 1 by water-rescue plot and Zuma/Rocky/Turbot cast.

lines: Okay snuggle in, story about the rescue puppies, the ones in the tall tower.
So one day there's a kid named Rider, he's a boy with the cool four-wheeler.
And he has all his dogs and they each have a special truck.
Like which one?
The spotted one, the firefighter, his name is Marshmallow.
Marshall!
Right Marshall, he's clumsy, he, he slides down the pole and bumps everybody.
And there's a chase pup, he's the police one with the spotlight and the cones.
So a baby goat wandered off up on the farmer's hill.
And it's stuck on a ledge and can't get down, baa baa.
Oh no the goat!
So Rider taps his watch and says puppies to the tower.
And the flying one, she does a backflip, she loves backflips.
She zooms up in her helicopter and hovers over the ledge real careful.
Then the police pup shoots his net up so the goat lands soft.
Did they get him?
Yep, soft landing, baby goat is safe with his mama.
And they all bark and Rider says nobody too small.
Okay milk's almost gone, lie back.
stories.tell_note: Franchise not named; Marshall + Chase as 'chase pup' + Ryder mangled to 'Rider'; lots of generic action; recognizable but lighter.

lines: Come here, I'll do the puppy team one, the ones that live in the bay.
So the town is having the big festival down by the water.
And the mayor lady carries her chicken everywhere in her purse, the chicken's name is funny.
Chickaletta!
Yeah Chick-a-letta, that silly hen, she pops her head out the bag.
So the chicken hops out and waddles right onto the merry-go-round.
And it starts spinning and the chicken's going round and round, bawk bawk.
Then the boy leader calls his dogs, he says no job too big.
So the little pink flying pup, Sky, she lifts off to look.
And the digging pup brings his shovel truck to slow the ride down.
Wait what's the digging one called?
Rubble, the gray one, he loves snacks and naps.
So Sky swoops down and grabs the chicken gentle in her claw thing.
And sets her right back in the mayor's bag, safe and sound.
Bawk!
And everybody cheers and they eat festival pie.
Okay that's it, eyes closed, no more spinning chickens.
stories.tell_note: Franchise not named; Skye mangled to 'Sky', Rubble + Chickaletta + Mayor Goodway implied; chicken-on-carousel filler keeps it medium; differentiated from story 3 by festival/chicken plot.

lines: Okay last one, lie down. There's a town by the sea and a boy who helps people.
He's not a grownup, he's like a big kid, and he rides a quad bike everywhere.
And he's got a team that comes when things go wrong.
What kind of team?
Helpers, with little trucks, each one does a different job, you know that.
So one morning the train was coming and a tree fell on the tracks.
And the conductor couldn't stop in time, choo choo, getting close.
Uh oh, what did they do?
So the boy taps his wrist gadget and says, alright everybody, to the tower.
And the strong one, the build pup, um, Ruddle I think, he brings the wrecking claw.
And the police helper set out the orange cones and the net first.
Then they lift the big tree right off the rails.
Then what?
They cleared the whole tree just before the train got there, screech, it stopped.
And the conductor waved thank you out the window.
And the boy goes, see, when you work together you can do anything.
Was anybody hurt?
Nope, everybody safe, even the squirrels in the tree.
Okay, that's the end, goodnight, no more questions.
stories.tell_note: Subtle: Ryder archetype (kid leader, quad bike, wrist gadget, tower, helper pups with trucks) plus one buried mis-transcribed canonical name, Rubble mangled to 'Ruddle'; no franchise named; could read generic if careless.

lines: You want the spotty dog one? Okay, scoot over.
So there's this puppy and he's white with black spots all over.
And he's a fire dog, he rides the red truck with the ladder.
Like a dalmatian?
Yeah like that, but he's clumsy, he always trips coming down the pole.
His name's kind of like a swamp, Marsh-something.
Marsh-all?
That's it. So the school bell tower had a fire alarm going off, beep beep.
And it was just a cat that knocked something over, no real fire.
But the spotty pup didn't know, so he rolls up with the hose and the ladder.
And he sprays a little bit and trips over his own paws, splash.
Did he get wet?
Soaked himself, yeah, water all over, but he climbed up and got the cat.
And it turned out fine, false alarm, everybody laughed.
And the boy on the four-wheeler patted his head and said good job, pup.
He says that a lot huh.
Mm-hmm, every time. Okay eyes shut now.
Just one more after?
No. Sleep.
stories.tell_note: Subtle: Marshall identified by description and 'Marsh-all' near-mangle plus the dalmatian fire-pup and quad-bike kid; franchise never named; one tell, buried.
